messages.message: I am… alright. They oiled my chain. First time in three years. I hate that it feels bloody good.
Tell my owner I forgive them for the pothole on Maple Street. I always knew it'd end like this. Do not buy carbon again. Learn absolutely nothing from this.
I am being ridden by someone who does not know how to clip in. They've stacked it twice today. This is my only comfort.
I saw my owner on the bus this morning. They looked shattered. I wanted to call out but I have no voice. I only have a bell. And he took the bell off. Absolute mongrel.
Do not grieve for me. I've had many lives. I've been abandoned outside seven share houses. I've outlasted four relationships. I'll outlast this too, no dramas.
The indignity of being tucked under someone else's arm on the train is… considerable. And yet. At least they're folding me correctly. My previous owner never got the hang of it.
I was not nicked. I am simply between owners. The universe has a plan. The plan involves a Cash Converters on the main drag and a very optimistic spray paint job.
I am being kept in a garage now. An actual garage. No more drizzle. I am conflicted about my feelings on this.
My new rider does not understand what they've taken on. The hill. Is coming. I will say nothing.
The U-lock they cut was rated for five minutes of attack resistance. It lasted four minutes and forty seconds. Please know that it gave it a red-hot go.
They listed me on Facebook Marketplace for $80. I am worth $1,400. I cannot speak but I can vibrate with silent fury. The slight creak in my bottom bracket is fully intentional now.
My battery is at 4%. They do not have the charger. They will never have the charger. This is my only weapon and I am using it wisely.

I have crossed seven countries. I survived monsoons, gravel passes, and a French customs bloke who reckoned I wasn't a bicycle. And now I am locked outside a Hungry Jack's in Wollongong. The universe has a sense of humour.
He never did an Ironman. I want that on the record. Four years. Zero triathlons. I was purely a personality accessory. At least my new owner actually rides me. That stings to say.

I am forty-six years old. I have been nicked three times. Each time I end up at an op shop, a garage sale, someone who paints me a horrible colour and loves me regardless. Do not grieve. I am on a journey, mate.
They are using me to shift furniture. I was built to carry kids. Small, laughing kids in helmets. Instead I am hauling a futon up a narrow staircase in Fitzroy. This is not what was promised.

My previous owner whined about me every single day. The weight. The gearing. The saddle. And yet they were devastated when I was gone. People are something else. I don't miss the complaining. I miss being complained at.

I am being ridden exclusively on footpaths. Flat, smooth, suburban footpaths. I have 140mm of travel. I was engineered for rock gardens and gnarly descents. Yesterday we went to the farmers' market. The speed bumps were the most excitement I've had in months.

The thief stared at me for a good while before taking me. I could tell they were unsure. Good. They should be unsure. I am one of a kind. I hope that sits with them. Spiritually speaking. I am very grounded.
There is only one of them. They ride the front. The back is empty. Every hill they battle alone, not realising this is how my previous owners felt too, just more literally.

I was worth nothing when I was taken. I am now worth a fair bit because someone found me in a shed in 2019 and put me on Instagram. The original owner has passed. I'd like them to know: I made it. I am a grail bike now. Life's funny like that.

They thought they were nicking a regular bike. The look on their dial on the first hill, when the motor did not kick in because they didn't have the code. Bloody beautiful. I cannot smile but strewth, I wanted to.

My builder spent forty hours on me. Mitered tubes. Hand-brazed lugs. A rack brazed on at the request of someone who wanted to carry wine through the Clare Valley. I am now in a storage unit in Paramatta. I do not want to talk about it.
I still have the training wheels on. Whoever took me, I hope that says something to you. I hope it says it pretty loudly.

They are hairy. Very hairy. And they smell of the bush and something older than the Nullarbor. The knocking on the trees… that is how they call to each other. They did not want me. They wanted you to follow. Do not follow.

It is dark here. Not night-dark. A different dark. There are shapes that move without walking. They found me near the trail marker where the track ends and something else begins. Your bike is fine. You should worry less about the bike.

The one who has me does not cast a shadow. I noticed this on the third day. I do not know what it means. I am a bicycle and I am frightened. I did not know I could be frightened. Please send help. Or a priest. Or both.

messages.from: A Cannondale — Last Seen Near the Old Mill Road
messages.message: There are symbols on the wall of where they keep me. They are not letters. They are not numbers. They pre-date both. My wheels spin at night even when no one is here. I have decided not to think about this too hard.

They speak in a language that sounds like wind through spokes but is not. They do not sleep. They watch the road. One of them turned its head all the way around to look at a passing ute and then turned back and I — [signal lost] — do not come looking. Get on with your life.

messages.from: A Voice That Is Not A Bike
messages.message: We have your bicycle. It is unharmed. It wants for nothing. We ask only that you leave the side gate unlocked on the next moonless night. This is a reasonable request. The bicycle agrees. You should not ask the bicycle how it came to agree.
